items.title: Trump compares US strikes on Iran to WWII Pearl Harbor attack
items.standfirst: During Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit to Washington, Donald #Trump was asked why #US allies - including #Japan - were not involved in talks regarding strikes in #Iran before they happened. Trump said the reason was surprise, before asking why Japan hadn’t warned of the attack on Pearl Harbor.
items.content: During Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit to Washington, Donald #Trump was asked why #US allies - including #Japan - were not involved in talks regarding strikes in #Iran before they happened. Trump said the reason was surprise, before asking why Japan hadn’t warned of the attack on Pearl Harbor.

items.title: Trump mocks Japan about Pearl Harbor in response to question about Iran war

items.content: In a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister, US President Donald Trump was asked why he didn't inform allies about his plan to attack Iran.

items.title: West Point analysis warns that strait of Hormuz blockade will strangle US defense industry
items.standfirst: Report shows how minerals critical to defense readiness have seen a ‘near total’ disruption in seaborne tradeMiddle East crisis – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe closure of the strait of Hormuz is causing a “paralyzing, real-time problem” for any prospective manufacturing surge in the US defense industrial base, and even for the repair of defense equipment damaged by Iranian attacks, according to analysis published by West Point’s Modern War Institute.In particular sulphur, a vital upstream input in the extraction of critical minerals including copper and cobalt, has seen a “near total” disruption of seaborne trade in the straits, which makes up half the world’s total shipments, and prices have spiked nearly 25% since the war began, and seen a 165% rise year on year, the report said. Continue reading...
items.content: Report shows how minerals critical to defense readiness have seen a ‘near total’ disruption in seaborne tradeMiddle East crisis – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe closure of the strait of Hormuz is causing a “paralyzing, real-time problem” for any prospective manufacturing surge in the US defense industrial base, and even for the repair of defense equipment damaged by Iranian attacks, according to analysis published by West Point’s Modern War Institute.In particular sulphur, a vital upstream input in the extraction of critical minerals including copper and cobalt, has seen a “near total” disruption of seaborne trade in the straits, which makes up half the world’s total shipments, and prices have spiked nearly 25% since the war began, and seen a 165% rise year on year, the report said. Continue reading...

items.title: Fed holds interest rates steady as Iran war drives up oil prices and inflation fears
items.standfirst: Jerome Powell resists Trump pressure as policymakers weigh energy shock against a weakening US jobs marketSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve held interest rates steady for the second time this year, a widely expected move amid turmoil in the Middle East and rising energy prices.Fed officials faced a confluence of issues to consider in their meeting this week: soaring oil and gas prices, fluctuating inflation that still remains above the Fed’s target of 2%, and a weakened job market that unexpectedly saw 92,000 losses last month. Continue reading...
items.content: Jerome Powell resists Trump pressure as policymakers weigh energy shock against a weakening US jobs marketSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve held interest rates steady for the second time this year, a widely expected move amid turmoil in the Middle East and rising energy prices.Fed officials faced a confluence of issues to consider in their meeting this week: soaring oil and gas prices, fluctuating inflation that still remains above the Fed’s target of 2%, and a weakened job market that unexpectedly saw 92,000 losses last month. clusters.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
clusters.neutral_headline: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: EU leaders vent anger after Hungary's Orban keeps blocking Ukraine loan
items.standfirst: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.
items.content: European Union leaders on Thursday lashed out at Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, accusing him of hijacking critical aid for Ukraine and undermining EU decision-making in an effort to win an election at home. France 24's correspondent Dave Keating has the latest from Brussels.

items.title: Outrage among EU leaders as Hungary's Orban U-turns on Ukraine loan
items.standfirst: Outraged EU leaders rounded on Viktor Orban after the Hungarian nationalist refused to lift his veto on funds for Ukraine's war effort at a Thursday summit, accusing him of a "gross act of disloyalty" tantamount to "blackmail." It is not the first time Orban has blocked aid to Ukraine, but the anger has been magnified by the fact the Hungarian leader had previously agreed to the deal, says France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.
items.content: Outraged EU leaders rounded on Viktor Orban after the Hungarian nationalist refused to lift his veto on funds for Ukraine's war effort at a Thursday summit, accusing him of a "gross act of disloyalty" tantamount to "blackmail." It is not the first time Orban has blocked aid to Ukraine, but the anger has been magnified by the fact the Hungarian leader had previously agreed to the deal, says France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.

items.title: Viktor Orbán refuses to agree to €90bn loan for Ukraine as EU leaders accuse him of betrayal
items.standfirst: German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as ‘a gross act of disloyalty’EU leaders fumed after Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, refused to drop his opposition to a vital €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, accusing him of betrayal and acting in bad faith.German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as “a gross act of disloyalty” adding: “I am firmly convinced that it will leave deep marks.” Continue reading...
items.content: German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as ‘a gross act of disloyalty’EU leaders fumed after Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, refused to drop his opposition to a vital €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, accusing him of betrayal and acting in bad faith.German chancellor Friedrich Merz described Orbán’s U-turn on the loan Hungary had agreed to in December as “a gross act of disloyalty” adding: “I am firmly convinced that it will leave deep marks.” Continue reading...

items.title: Household energy bills in Great Britain ‘could rise to almost £2,000 a year’ amid Iran war shock
items.standfirst: Consultancy forecasts typical £1,972 annual dual fuel bill as conflict pushes UK’s gas market past three-year highsBusiness live – latest updatesHousehold energy bills in Great Britain could soar by more than £330 a year to almost £2,000 from this summer after the war in Iran pushed the UK’s gas market past three-year highs.A typical combined household gas and electricity bill is now forecast to reach £1,972 a year from July under the UK government’s quarterly price cap, according to analysis by Cornwall Insight, an energy consultancy. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Muslims on Friday gathered to celebrate the Eid al-Fitr festival that marks the end of the holy month of Ramadan. FRANCE 24 looks at how communities across the world are coming together in prayer and celebration even as the US-Israeli war on Iran casts its shadow over the Middle East.

items.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'decimated,' Tehran claims only used ‘fraction’ of firepower
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel and the United States were "winning" the war against Iran, with the Islamic republic "decimated" and unable to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. However, Tehran is pushing back in this escalating war of words, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisting that Iran's military strength remains fully intact and will not faulter in the event of further attacks.
items.content: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that Israel and the United States were "winning" the war against Iran, with the Islamic republic "decimated" and unable to enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. However, Tehran is pushing back in this escalating war of words, with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisting that Iran's military strength remains fully intact and will not faulter in the event of further attacks.

items.title: Replay: Israeli PM Netanyahu addresses press conference
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Speaking at a press conference, Netanyahu declared, “We are winning, and Iran is being decimated,” highlighting the ongoing destruction of missile and nuclear weapons production facilities.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. items.content: Interim president announces changes after firing defence minister, who was close to Maduro, the leader ousted by US Venezuela’s interim president has said she has replaced all her senior military commanders, the latest in a flurry of changes since the US ousted Nicolás Maduro.Delcy Rodríguez announced the changes in a social media post a day after firing the long-serving defence minister, who had been close to Maduro, and replacing him with a former intelligence chief. Continue reading...

items.title: Venezuela interim president replaces top military commanders in sweeping reforms
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Thursday she had replaced all senior military commanders, expanding sweeping reforms after the US ousted Nicolas Maduro. The move follows the dismissal of a long-serving defence minister and the appointment of a former intelligence chief, as Caracas reshapes its armed forces.
items.content: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Thursday she had replaced all senior military commanders, expanding sweeping reforms after the US ousted Nicolas Maduro. The move follows the dismissal of a long-serving defence minister and the appointment of a former intelligence chief, as Caracas reshapes its armed forces.

clusters.title: Denmark sent explosives to blow up Greenland runways amid Trump invasion threat
clusters.neutral_headline: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
bullet_summary: Denmark sent soldiers and explosives to Greenland in January so that it could blow up runways in the event of a US invasion, the country's public broadcaster has said
Reported by Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Denmark sent explosives to blow up Greenland runways amid Trump invasion threat

items.content: Denmark sent soldiers and explosives to Greenland in January so that it could blow up runways in the event of a US invasion, the country's public broadcaster has said.

items.title: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
items.standfirst: Denmark's public broadcaster reports that the US operation to seize Venezuela's leader sparked fears it would act against Greenland.
items.content: Denmark's public broadcaster reports that the US operation to seize Venezuela's leader sparked fears it would act against Greenland.

clusters.title: Three charged in the US with smuggling AI chips into China

co-founder with illegally diverting billions of dollars in Nvidia Corp

items.content: US charges men reportedly tied to Super Micro Computer for helping to smuggle billions of dollars' worth of AI chips.

items.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Charged With Sending AI Tech to China
items.standfirst: The US charged a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder with illegally diverting billions of dollars in Nvidia Corp.-powered servers to China, initiating its highest-profile case against alleged smuggling of restricted AI technology to the Asian country.
items.content: The US charged a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder with illegally diverting billions of dollars in Nvidia Corp.-powered servers to China, initiating its highest-profile case against alleged smuggling of restricted AI technology to the Asian country.

clusters.neutral_headline: Nasa's Moon rocket Artemis rolls back to pad for possible April launch
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, CBS News

items.title: Nasa's Moon rocket Artemis rolls back to pad for possible April launch
items.standfirst: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.
items.content: Nasa is preparing a mission to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

items.title: Repaired moon rocket heads back to launch pad for April 1 blastoff
items.standfirst: After a trip back out to the launch pad, NASA's Artemis II rocket will be readied for a historic flight to the moon.
items.content: After a trip back out to the launch pad, NASA's Artemis II rocket will be readied for a historic flight to the moon.

clusters.title: U.S. Mint can begin to produce Trump commemorative gold coin
clusters.neutral_headline: U.S. Mint can begin to produce Trump commemorative gold coin
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, CBS News

items.title: U.S. Mint can begin to produce Trump commemorative gold coin
items.standfirst: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the Republican president, clears the way for the U.S. Mint to begin production on the coin, whose size and denomination are still under discussion.
items.content: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the Republican president, clears the way for the U.S. Mint to begin production on the coin, whose size and denomination are still under discussion.(Image credit: Alex Brandon)

items.title: Federal commission made up of Trump appointees approves design of his gold coin
items.standfirst: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the president and were appointed by him earlier this year, was without objection.
items.content: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the president and were appointed by him earlier this year, was without objection.

clusters.title: BTS release new album Arirang ahead of comeback concert
clusters.neutral_headline: BTS release new album Arirang ahead of comeback concert
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, France24

items.title: BTS release new album Arirang ahead of comeback concert
items.standfirst: Boyband drops album that speaks to its Korean roots ahead of Seoul comeback concert, with more than a quarter of a million fans expected to attendK-pop stars BTS released a new album on Friday billed as reflecting the maturing boy band’s Korean roots and identity, as buzz built ahead of their open-air comeback concert in the heart of Seoul.The Saturday night gig, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people, will be BTS’s first after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service. It comes ahead of an 82-date world tour. Continue reading...
items.content: Boyband drops album that speaks to its Korean roots ahead of Seoul comeback concert, with more than a quarter of a million fans expected to attendK-pop stars BTS released a new album on Friday billed as reflecting the maturing boy band’s Korean roots and identity, as buzz built ahead of their open-air comeback concert in the heart of Seoul.The Saturday night gig, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people, will be BTS’s first after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service. It comes ahead of an 82-date world tour. Continue reading...

items.title: K-pop megastars BTS drop new album ahead of comeback mega-concert
items.standfirst: After an almost four-year hiatus, K-pop icons BTS dropped a new album Friday ahead of an open-air concert in Seoul expected to draw more than a quarter of a million fans from around the world.
items.content: After an almost four-year hiatus, K-pop icons BTS dropped a new album Friday ahead of an open-air concert in Seoul expected to draw more than a quarter of a million fans from around the world.

clusters.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran war live: IRGC says spokesman killed in US-Israeli missile attack
bullet_summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported
Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Middle East war live: US-Israeli air strikes kill Iran’s Revolutionary Guard spokesperson
items.standfirst: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini was killed in dawn US-Israeli air strikes on Friday, state media reported. The strikes came as an Iranian drone attack hit Kuwait’s Mina al-Ahmadi oil refinery, causing several fires but no casualties. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran war live: IRGC says spokesman killed in US-Israeli missile attack
items.standfirst: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says its spokesperson has been killed in a US-Israeli missile attack.
items.content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says its spokesperson has been killed in a US-Israeli missile attack.

clusters.title: Iran hangs 3, including teen wrestler, in first executions over Jan. protests
clusters.neutral_headline: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests

Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say

clusters.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil and gas prices jump after Iran and Israel attack gasfields
bullet_summary: China must secure its energy supply “in its own hands”, its president was reported to have said during a visit to one of its vast oilfields in 2021
Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
items.standfirst: As other Asian economies race to conserve energy, China has huge reserves of oil and gas as well as alternative energy sources like wind and solarMiddle East crisis – live updatesXi Jinping has been preparing for a crisis like this for years. China must secure its energy supply “in its own hands”, its president was reported to have said during a visit to one of its vast oilfields in 2021.The US-Israel war on Iran plunged the Middle East into a deep conflict, with the strait of Hormuz – one of the most important waterways in global trade – all but closed and key energy facilities across the region under attack. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran says it will show ‘zero restraint’ if energy infrastructure is targeted again
items.standfirst: Foreign minister issues warning after Israeli attack on South Pars gasfield and as Qatar reels from retaliatory strike‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesIran said on Thursday it would show “zero restraint” if its energy infrastructure was targeted again as Qatar revealed that almost a fifth of its liquefied natural gas export capacity had been knocked out in an Iranian strike that is likely to have a years-long impact.The warning, delivered by the Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, followed Israel’s attack on Iran’s massive South Pars gasfield – which it shares with Qatar – which triggered Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas complex and other Gulf neighbours, sending stock markets tumbling globally and triggering sharp increases in gas prices. Continue reading...
items.content: Foreign minister issues warning after Israeli attack on South Pars gasfield and as Qatar reels from retaliatory strike‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesIran said on Thursday it would show “zero restraint” if its energy infrastructure was targeted again as Qatar revealed that almost a fifth of its liquefied natural gas export capacity had been knocked out in an Iranian strike that is likely to have a years-long impact.The warning, delivered by the Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, followed Israel’s attack on Iran’s massive South Pars gasfield – which it shares with Qatar – which triggered Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas complex and other Gulf neighbours, sending stock markets tumbling globally and triggering sharp increases in gas prices. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Doomsday scenario’: a visual guide to the oil and gas site attacks in the Middle East
items.standfirst: Attacks on facilities by both sides in the conflict this week threaten grave consequences for the global economyMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe escalating attacks on key oil and gas projects in the Middle East are expected to fuel a new phase of the ongoing conflict, with profound consequences for the world’s energy supplies and the global economy.The Iran regime has vowed to target a string of key energy infrastructure across the region after warning that an Israeli strike on a production facility for its largest gasfield at South Pars on Wednesday had ignited a “full-scale economic war”. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Oil and gas prices jump after Iran and Israel attack gasfields
items.standfirst: State-run QatarEnergy says Iranian attack on its facilities has wiped out 17% of its LNG capacity for up to five yearsMiddle East crisis – live updatesBusiness live – latest updatesGas prices jumped to four-year highs and oil prices rose again after an escalation of attacks by Israel and Iran on gasfields heightened fears of prolonged disruption to international energy supplies.QatarEnergy told Reuters on Thursday that Iran had damaged facilities that produced 17% of the state-owned company’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) export capacity and that it would take three to five years to repair them. Continue reading...
items.content: State-run QatarEnergy says Iranian attack on its facilities has wiped out 17% of its LNG capacity for up to five yearsMiddle East crisis – live updatesBusiness live – latest updatesGas prices jumped to four-year highs and oil prices rose again after an escalation of attacks by Israel and Iran on gasfields heightened fears of prolonged disruption to international energy supplies.QatarEnergy told Reuters on Thursday that Iran had damaged facilities that produced 17% of the state-owned company’s liquefied natural gas (LNG) export capacity and that it would take three to five years to repair them. Continue reading...
